macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Inflation Measures Diverge Sharply as AI Demand Drives Chip Price Surge
items.summary: Core PCE inflation has surged to 4.1% in February 2026 while core CPI remains subdued, creating a historic 60 basis point gap driven largely by AI-related microchip demand colliding with Iran-related supply shocks. This divergence complicates the Fed's rate-cut outlook and signals structural inflationary pressures.

items.title: Trump's Tariffs Have Done 'Significant Damage' to U.S. Economy, Moody's Chief Economist Says
items.summary: Mark Zandi reports that Liberation Day tariffs have stalled job growth and accelerated inflation to 3% year-over-year, with the consumer expenditure deflator well above the Fed's 2% target. Combined with rising energy costs from the Iran conflict, economic headwinds are intensifying.

items.title: Fed Officials Warn Iran War Could Trigger Prolonged Inflation Shock
items.summary: Federal Reserve officials express growing concern that elevated oil prices and supply chain disruptions from the U.S.-Iran conflict pose persistent threats to price stability. These pressures could necessitate further interest rate hikes and extended periods of elevated inflation.

items.title: Powell's Tenure Marked by Policy Errors and Structural Inflation Challenges, Says El-Erian
items.summary: Mohamed El-Erian critiques outgoing Fed Chair Powell's record, noting inflation has remained structurally above the 2% target for six consecutive years and warning the economy faces several more years of above-target inflation even before recent geopolitical volatility.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Zcash Surges 30-40% in 24 Hours on Multicoin Capital Position and Quantum Resistance Narrative
items.summary: Zcash (ZEC) jumped 30-40% in 24 hours after Multicoin Capital disclosed a significant position at Consensus Miami and the FCMP++ upgrade entered its next testnet phase on May 6. The rally reflects growing interest in quantum-resistant cryptography and privacy features, with ZEC now trading near $570, up 122% over the past month.

items.title: XRP Surges Past $1.45 Following RLUSD Milestone and XRP Ledger Tokenization
items.summary: XRP rose above $1.45 after Ripple's RLUSD stablecoin crossed $1 billion in supply and tokenized STRC launched on the XRP Ledger network, adding over $1 billion to the asset's market cap in minutes.

items.title: Bitcoin Oscillates Between $80,000-$82,700 Amid US-Iran Deal Optimism and Technical Resistance
items.summary: Bitcoin surged to $82,700 on optimism surrounding a potential US-Iran deal that eased energy market concerns, but retreated to $80,000 as doubts emerged. The cryptocurrency is currently trading around $82,039 as dollar weakness provides support.

items.title: Crypto Futures Market Experiences $621 Million in Liquidations Over 24 Hours
items.summary: The cryptocurrency derivatives market faced significant turbulence with $621 million in futures liquidations, with Bitcoin leading at $33.5 million, followed by Ethereum at $13.8 million, reflecting heightened market volatility.

items.title: Toncoin and Near Protocol Emerge as Top Performers Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Toncoin and Near Protocol led market gains over the past 24 hours as total liquidations exceeded $500 million, suggesting continued short-term volatility in crypto markets.

items.title: Core Scientific Acquires Polaris for $421 Million to Pivot Bitcoin Mining Toward AI Data Centers
items.summary: Core Scientific announced a $421 million acquisition of Polaris to transition its Bitcoin mining operations toward AI data center infrastructure, reflecting industry shift toward AI-related services.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Unveils 'Code with Claude' Features Including Scheduled Routines and 'Dreams' Memory System
items.summary: Anthropic announced major updates at the 'Code with Claude' event, featuring scheduled AI routines, outcome-based agents, multi-agent orchestration, a new 'Dreams' memory system for long-term agent behavior, and higher rate limits, poised to impact AI product development.

items.title: Enterprise AI Adoption Triggers Unexpected Budget Crises from Token Usage in Agentic Workflows
items.summary: Experts discuss how AI scaling beyond pilots leads to massive cloud bills, with single prompts triggering dozens of API calls and agentic workflows burning tokens rapidly, urging better financial planning and governance.

items.title: 2026 Research Paper Warns of 'AI Layoff Trap' and Economic Risks from Widespread Automation
items.summary: A UPenn and Boston University paper analyzes how AI-driven layoffs create a Prisoner's Dilemma, potentially damaging the economy as companies compete to automate, with discussions on UBI limitations and policy solutions like Pigouvian taxes.

items.title: AI CEOs Express Fears Over AGI Risks, Alignment, and Weaponization Potential
items.summary: Video explores why AI leaders are wary of AGI, covering alignment problems, researcher exodus from OpenAI, job disruption via recursive self-improvement, and human weaponization risks.

items.title: Global South Poised to Lead Sustainable AI Through Intellectual Capital, Not Massive Hardware
items.summary: AI scientist Randy Goebel argues that large model sustainability issues offer opportunities for the Global South to innovate via open science, machine learning, and explainable AI, bypassing capital-intensive paths.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: New Legendary Champion Tekteon Fissureflesh Released in Raid Shadow Legends
items.summary: Raid Shadow Legends has released a new legendary champion, Tekteon Fissureflesh, which is being described as one of the most dangerous and top-tier champions the game has ever released.

items.title: The Division 2 Developers Confirm Broken Targeted Loot Bug with Fix Incoming
items.summary: Developers have confirmed a broken targeted loot system in The Division 2 and announced that a fix is incoming, advising players not to farm affected loot.

items.title: Raid Shadow Legends Major Update Released with Surprises
items.summary: Raid Shadow Legends has released a significant update day with multiple changes and features, including some unexpected additions.
items.source: YouTube - Raid Shadow Legends News & Guides

items.title: Windrose Open World Survival Game Features New Ship Crafting
items.summary: Windrose, a new open-world pirate survival game, allows players to explore biomes and craft ships like the frigate in its swamp areas.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: US Tech Sector Rebounds Strongly on US-Iran Peace Expectations and AI Chip Earnings
items.summary: The US tech sector and broader market surged in the past 24 hours, driven by optimism over US-Iran negotiations including potential Strait of Hormuz reopening, with AMD and Super Micro's strong reports pushing Nasdaq and S&P 500 to new highs.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX Terafab Chip Plant Estimated at $119 Billion
items.summary: SpaceX's planned Terafab chip plant represents major vertical integration in AI hardware supply chain, bolstering US manufacturing in semiconductors and space technology.

items.title: Apple to Pay $250 Million Settlement for Delayed AI-Powered Siri Delivery
items.summary: Apple has agreed to a proposed $250 million settlement in a California class action lawsuit over failing to deliver its promised AI-powered Siri features on time.

items.title: Snap's $400 Million Deal with Perplexity AI Officially Dead
items.summary: Snap's planned $400 million partnership with Perplexity for an AI search feature in Snapchat has collapsed, as the feature never fully launched.

items.title: Chrome Downloads 4GB AI File Without User Consent, Researcher Alleges
items.summary: A researcher claims Google Chrome automatically downloads a 4GB AI-related file without permission, though users can toggle it off easily.

items.title: Nuro Approved for Driverless Uber Robotaxi Tests on California Roads
items.summary: Nuro and Uber gain approval to test fully autonomous robotaxis on California public roads, with driverless operations planned to start later this year.
